level_signals: Base salary range $151,200–$189,000 consistent with mid-senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2-3+ Years of TPM Experience: Proven track record of managing complex, cross-functional technical programs, ideally in robotics, hardware, or large-scale data operations.
Strong Technical Foundation: A degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, combined with a deep understanding of AI/ML.
Hands-on Problem Solving Skills: A proactive approach to overcoming technical and logistical roadblocks in a fast-paced environment.
Operational Grit: Experience "in the weeds" of operational challenges - you don't just identify bottlenecks; you build the solution to clear them.
Entrepreneurial Mindset: You are comfortable with ambiguity and excited about building infrastructure from scratch in a fast-paced environment.

Drive Multi-Project Outcomes: Serve as the primary driver for a portfolio of robotics projects, tracking milestones, deliverables, and dependencies to ensure on-time delivery for the world's leading AI researchers.

Architect Scalable Pipelines: Partner with engineering to design and optimize end-to-end data ingestion pipelines, ensuring they can handle the high-fidelity sensor data (video, logs, teleop) required for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
Enforce Data Quality & Governance: Define KPIs and Quality Assurance (QA) metrics to ensure datasets are high-fidelity, labeled correctly, and compliant with rigorous privacy and safety standards.
Standardize for Scale: Develop and refine Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that allow us to scale collection efforts across global, distributed teams.
Communicate with Precision: Provide regular, data-driven progress updates to Scale's executive team, utilizing SQL and dashboards to monitor performance and guide strategic pivots.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: SQL Proficiency: Ability to pull your own data and build your own tracking dashboards.
Global Experience: Experience interacting with distributed teams or collection sites across different time zones.
Robotics Domain Expertise: Familiarity with robotic hardware, sensor calibration, or the nuances of collecting data for Physical AI.
AI/CV Knowledge: Familiarity with Computer Vision or Large Language Model (LLM) training requirements.

scope.surface: Robotics Data business unit — portfolio of robotics data collection and ingestion programs driving $XXM+ in new revenue
scope.business_model: B2B data services and AI infrastructure for enterprise and government clients
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of robotics data milestones
Dataset quality / KPI and QA metrics
core_responsibilities: Serve as the primary driver for a portfolio of robotics projects, tracking milestones, deliverables, and dependencies
Act as the central liaison between AI clients, hardware engineers, and global operational teams
Partner with engineering to design and optimize end-to-end data ingestion pipelines
Define KPIs and QA metrics to ensure high-fidelity, correctly labeled, compliant datasets
Develop and refine SOPs to scale collection efforts across global distributed teams
Provide data-driven progress updates to Scale's executive team using SQL and dashboards

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Clearly defined product surface and ownership (Robotics Data business unit with $XXM+ revenue impact)
Equity compensation mentioned in addition to base salary
Specific and concrete responsibilities listed — not vague
Explicit call out of career progression ('accelerated career progression')
Business unit described as seeing 'historic levels of growth'
red_flags: Role appears to require onsite presence in SF, NYC, or Seattle — no remote option stated
Visa sponsorship policy not addressed in the JD
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for the same role adds friction for reapplicants
The JD mixes 'Ideally, you'd have' language (suggesting preferred) with items that read as hard requirements (e.g. 2-3+ years TPM experience, degree) — classification ambiguity exists
'$XXM+ in new revenue' suggests revenue figures are redacted, reducing transparency about actual scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false; JD names specific cities with pay range and no remote mention)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and client list)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not directly stated)
ambiguities: All requirements appear under 'Ideally, you'd have' heading which could suggest preferred, but items like '2-3+ Years of TPM Experience' and degree requirement read as hard minimums. Classified as hard (must_haves) given unconditional framing and seniority context.
Visa sponsorship status is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Set to 'unclear'.
Office policy (hybrid vs onsite) is not explicitly stated; the JD only provides pay ranges for SF, NYC, and Seattle with no remote option mentioned. Treated as onsite.
